novel_features.name: Markup drift audit
novel_features.description: Find every material and equipment SKU whose actual markup has drifted off the materials-markup tier ladder.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining synced materials and equipment against the markup tier ladder locally; no single ServiceTitan API call returns tier-vs-actual markup.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this after any vendor cost change to catch SKUs that are now mispriced for their tier before margin erodes.

novel_features.name: Cost-drift report
novel_features.description: Show every SKU whose cost moved since a given date, with old and new cost, old and new price, and whether the price followed.
novel_features.rationale: Reads the local sku_cost_history snapshot table; the ServiceTitan API exposes no cost history at all.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to see whether recent vendor cost increases were actually passed through to price.

novel_features.name: Missing vendor-part audit
novel_features.description: List materials and equipment whose primary vendor part number is empty — the missing 2M Part # sweep.
novel_features.rationale: A null-filter over the primaryVendor.vendorPart field of synced SKUs; the ServiceTitan API has no query for absent vendor parts.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to enforce the 2M Part # discipline before a vendor-quote reconcile, which matches on vendor part.

novel_features.name: Warranty attribution lint
novel_features.description: Flag equipment whose warranty text is not prefixed Manufacturer's, or that is missing JKA's 1-year parts & labor line.
novel_features.rationale: Text-pattern lint over synced equipment warranty and description fields against ServiceTitan-pricebook-specific attribution rules.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to keep warranty attribution honest — manufacturer warranties clearly labeled, JKA's own offering listed alongside.

novel_features.name: Orphan SKU finder
novel_features.description: List materials, equipment, and services assigned to inactive or non-existent categories.
novel_features.rationale: Joins synced SKUs against the categories table locally; the ServiceTitan API cannot filter SKUs by their category's active state.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this during category taxonomy cleanup to find SKUs that fell out of the visible tree.

novel_features.name: Vendor quote reconcile
novel_features.description: Match a vendor cost file against synced SKUs by vendor part number and print a no-write diff of proposed cost changes; accepts CSV or Claude-extracted JSON from a quote, order confirmation, or invoice PDF.
novel_features.rationale: Self-healing matches an external file against primaryVendor and otherVendors part numbers in the local store; pure local join, no write unless --apply is passed.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a vendor quote, order confirmation, or invoice lands; have Claude extract it to JSON, then reconcile against current costs before touching the pricebook.

novel_features.name: Hold-markup repricer
novel_features.description: Compute the tier-correct price for markup-drifted SKUs and emit the exact update payloads; preview by default, --apply to write.
novel_features.rationale: Depends on the markup-audit tier math and the local markup ladder; emits ServiceTitan-shaped update bodies no generic client would build.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this after markup-audit to apply hold-markup pricing in one pass instead of editing each SKU by hand.

novel_features.name: Pricebook health summary
novel_features.description: One compact rollup of markup-drift, vendor-part-gap, warranty-lint, cost-drift, orphan-SKU, duplicate, and weak-copy counts.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates every local audit into a single agent-shaped JSON blob sized for priming; no API equivalent exists.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this first in any pricebook session to see what needs attention before drilling into a specific audit.

novel_features.name: Bulk-write planner
novel_features.description: Turn a reviewed cost or copy change file into a single pricebook bulk-update payload instead of N individual update calls.
novel_features.rationale: Transforms a local reconcile, reprice, or copy-audit diff into one ServiceTitan bulk-operation body; matters under ST's ~7k/hr rate limit.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to apply a batch of reconciled changes in one rate-limit-friendly call.

novel_features.name: Duplicate-SKU detector
novel_features.description: Cluster near-duplicate materials and equipment so excess pricebook growth can be collapsed.
novel_features.rationale: Fuzzy-matches synced SKUs against each other on normalized code, display name, description, and vendor part in local SQLite; the ServiceTitan API has no find-SKUs-like-this query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find redundant parts before they multiply — duplicate SKUs scatter estimates and inventory across entries that should be one.

novel_features.name: Sales-copy description audit
novel_features.description: Flag SKUs whose display name or description is empty, too short, ALL-CAPS, a bare part number, or otherwise not customer-facing.
novel_features.rationale: Service-specific content-quality lint over synced SKU text fields; a sales-copy agent rewrites the flagged entries and materials update or bulk-plan writes them back.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find pricebook entries that read like internal shorthand instead of customer-facing sales copy.

novel_features.name: Natural-language part finder
novel_features.description: Forgiving ranked search over synced SKUs tuned for describing a part you do not know the code for.
novel_features.rationale: Domain-tuned multi-field ranking across code, display name, description, category, and vendor part in the local store, with a tech-facing output shape; more than the generic FTS search command.
novel_features.example: servicetitan-pricebook-pp-cli find "1 hp submersible pump motor" --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a tech describes a part in plain words — it returns suggested SKUs with the code, price, vendor part, and category they need to pick one.

narrative.headline: A focused per-module ServiceTitan CLI for the Pricebook — sync once, then audit markup, costs, vendor parts, and warranties the ST UI cannot.
narrative.value_prop: This CLI (servicetitan-pricebook-pp-cli) mirrors every ServiceTitan Pricebook v2 endpoint (categories, client-specific pricing, discounts & fees, equipment, materials, materials markup, bulk operations, images, services, export feeds) and adds twelve novel commands that join across them in a local SQLite store. It snapshots cost and price history on every sync, so markup-audit, cost-drift, and reprice become one-shot. It is part of a per-module ST CLI family (servicetitan-crm, servicetitan-dispatch, servicetitan-inventory, servicetitan-jpm, servicetitan-pricebook) designed to replace the heavy 600+-tool general ServiceTitan MCP with focused, agent-native binaries.
narrative.auth_narrative: ServiceTitan uses composed auth: a static ST-App-Key header (set ST_APP_KEY) plus an OAuth2 client-credentials bearer token (set ST_CLIENT_ID and ST_CLIENT_SECRET). Run servicetitan-pricebook-pp-cli auth login to walk the credential setup, then set the three credentials plus ST_TENANT_ID (your numeric tenant ID) in your environment. The client mints and refreshes the bearer token automatically and attaches both headers to every call. Whitespace is stripped defensively from credentials — a known JKA gotcha where a trailing newline in an env file produced opaque invalid_client 400s.

quickstart.comment: Confirm ST_APP_KEY, bearer token, ST_TENANT_ID, and base URL before anything else.
Pull materials, equipment, services, categories, markup tiers, and rate sheets into the local store and snapshot cost/price history.
See markup-drift, vendor-part-gap, warranty, cost-drift, and orphan counts in one rollup.
Drill into the SKUs whose markup has drifted off the tier ladder.
Diff a vendor quote against current pricebook costs before writing anything back.

troubleshoots.fix: Check for trailing whitespace or newlines in ST_CLIENT_ID / ST_CLIENT_SECRET; the client strips them, but verify the env var was set cleanly.

troubleshoots.symptom: every call returns 401 JWT not present
troubleshoots.fix: Run doctor — if the bearer token is missing, confirm ST_CLIENT_ID and ST_CLIENT_SECRET are set; the client mints the token on first call.
Confirm ST_TENANT_ID is set to your numeric tenant ID; every pricebook path is tenant-scoped and an unset tenant silently yields no data.
Run sync first — these commands read the local store and the sku_cost_history snapshot table, not the live API.
ServiceTitan allows ~7,000 req/hr per app key per environment; the client backs off automatically — re-run sync or use --resources to scope it.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or operator needs to inspect or maintain the ServiceTitan Pricebook — costs, prices, markup tiers, vendor part numbers, warranty text, category taxonomy — without loading the 600+-tool general ST MCP. It is the right choice for margin-discipline checks after vendor cost changes, vendor-quote reconciliation, and pricebook hygiene audits. Run sync first; the novel audit commands read the local store.

recipes.title: Post-vendor-quote margin check
recipes.explanation: After a sync, list every SKU whose realised markup drifted more than 5 points off its cost tier — the margin check to run after a vendor cost change.

recipes.title: Reconcile a 2M vendor quote
recipes.explanation: Match the quote's vendor part numbers against synced SKUs and print the proposed cost diff without writing.

recipes.title: Narrow a large material list for an agent
recipes.explanation: Materials responses are large and deeply nested; --agent with --select dotted paths returns only the fields an agent needs to reason about cost and vendor part. Pass your tenant ID as the first argument.

recipes.title: Pre-session pricebook triage
recipes.explanation: One rollup of every audit count so an agent knows where to look before drilling in.

recipes.title: Find SKUs missing a 2M Part #
recipes.explanation: List materials whose primary vendor part number is empty so they can be filled before the next reconcile.

trigger_phrases: find duplicate parts in the pricebook
find SKUs missing a 2M part number
